# Sitemap auto-generated by Jaspr build

## Context and Problem Statement

The site needs a `sitemap.xml` for search engine discoverability. The site has a large number of dynamic routes (one per app, company, and developer) that would require manual maintenance if the sitemap were a static file.

## Decision Drivers

- The sitemap must include all routes, including dynamically generated ones for every app, company, and developer detail page
- Manual maintenance of a static file is error-prone and will drift out of sync
- The solution should require minimal ongoing effort

## Considered Options

- Static `web/sitemap.xml` checked into the repo (only covers fixed routes)
- A build script that generates `sitemap.xml` from the route list
- Jaspr's built-in `--sitemap-domain` flag on `jaspr build`

## Decision Outcome

Chosen option: **Jaspr's built-in sitemap generation**, because it automatically enumerates all `Route` definitions, including dynamically generated ones for every made-in entry.

The CI build command is `dart run jaspr_cli:jaspr build --sitemap-domain https://flutterbelgium.be`. Static routes are annotated with `RouteSettings(changeFreq, priority)` to control sitemap metadata. Dynamic detail page routes inherit default values.

### Consequences

- Good, because the sitemap always matches the actual routes with zero manual effort
- Good, because dynamic routes (per app, company, developer) are included automatically
- Good, because `RouteSettings` allows fine-grained control of `changefreq` and `priority` per route
- Neutral, because the domain must be kept up to date in the CI workflow if the domain ever changes
